title: dualActiveBridge
description: Dual Active Bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter excitation. Modulation parameters follow the standard Triple Phase Shift (TPS) convention: D1 = primary-bridge intra-leg shift, D2 = secondary-bridge intra-leg shift, D3 = inter-bridge (outer) shift. See Huang et al., Energies 11(9):2419 (2018) and Maniktala & Rosano, 'Power Supplies A to Z', 3rd ed. (Ch. 2).

inputVoltage.description: The input voltage of the DAB converter

seriesInductance.description: The series inductance for power transfer control. If 0, uses transformer leakage inductance

useLeakageInductance.description: Whether to use transformer leakage inductance as the series inductor

perSecondaryLeakage.description: Optional per-secondary leakage inductance in H, one entry per output. Required for physically meaningful multi-output DAB simulation; if absent, all secondaries share the same series inductance.

dabOperatingPoint.description: One DAB operating point. Modulation is parameterised by three phase-shift variables D1, D2, D3 (Huang et al. 2018 convention): D1 and D2 are intra-bridge (leg-to-leg) shifts that control each bridge's effective duty cycle, and D3 is the outer inter-bridge shift that controls power transfer. SPS uses D3 only (D1=D2=0). EPS uses D3 + one inner shift (D1, with D2=0). DPS uses D3 + one symmetric inner shift (D1=D2). TPS uses all three independently.
dabOperatingPoint.title: dabOperatingPoint

outputVoltages.description: A list of output voltages, one per output

outputVoltagesType.description: Type of value carried in outputVoltages: which aggregate of the periodic waveform the number represents. Defaults to dc. See IEV 103-02.

outputCurrents.description: A list of output currents, one per output

outputCurrentsType.description: Type of value carried in outputCurrents: which aggregate of the periodic waveform the number represents. Defaults to dc. See IEV 103-02.

switchingFrequency.description: The switching frequency of the operating point

ambientTemperature.description: The ambient temperature of the operating point

modulationType.description: DAB modulation scheme. SPS (Single Phase Shift): only D3 is non-zero. EPS (Extended Phase Shift): D1>0 on primary bridge, D2=0, D3 controls power. DPS (Dual Phase Shift): D1=D2>0 symmetric, D3 controls power. TPS (Triple Phase Shift): D1, D2, D3 independent. Ref: Huang et al., Energies 11(9):2419 (2018).

innerPhaseShift1.description: D1 — primary-bridge intra-leg phase shift in degrees (shift between the two legs of the primary full-bridge). Range [0, 90). 0 = square wave (50% duty). Used in EPS, DPS, TPS.

innerPhaseShift2.description: D2 — secondary-bridge intra-leg phase shift in degrees (shift between the two legs of the secondary full-bridge). Range [0, 90). 0 = square wave (50% duty). Used in TPS. For DPS, if absent, defaults to D1 (symmetric).

innerPhaseShift3.description: D3 — inter-bridge (outer) phase shift in degrees between primary and secondary bridges. Signed: positive = forward power flow (primary → secondary). Used in all modulation modes. This is the primary power-transfer control variable.
